title: impatiens glauca var. glauca
intro: Impatiens glauca var. glauca, also known as Balsam Apple or Kalmegh, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are said to have a beneficial effect on various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Impatiens glauca var. glauca include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
